source.label: MARADMIN 051/23, Expansion of the Marine Corps Military Parental Leave Program

changes: MAX_PARENTAL_LEAVE_DAYS citation corrected from para 8 to para 11.d, which carries the verbatim 84-day enforcement language. Para 8 is 'Amplifying Instructions'.
ENTITLEMENT_WINDOW_DAYS citation corrected from para 8.b.(2) to para 8.c.(3), the forfeiture-after-1-year rule.
MIN_INCREMENT_DAYS confirmed at para 8.a.(1)(b); status UNVERIFIED -> VERIFIED.

EVENT_PROXIMITY_MERGE confirmed at para 8.b.(1); status UNVERIFIED -> VERIFIED. Source states a 72-hour period, so the rule is re-expressed in hours (id EVENT_PROXIMITY_MERGE_HOURS, 72) rather than the prior approximation of 3 days.
Added MAX_INCREMENTS (12), stated in the same source line as the minimum increment.

rules.note: Commands and Marines are responsible for ensuring the Marine authorized this leave does not use over 84 days of parental leave, except in the circumstance identified in para 8.b.(2). The 84 days equals the 12-week entitlement stated as '12 weeks (84 days)' in para 7.a.(1).

Parental leave not executed before the expiration of 1 year from the date of the qualifying event is forfeited, unless para 8.a.(3) applies. The 1-year period runs from the date of birth, qualifying adoption, or placement (para 7); for separate multiple events it is measured from the later event (para 8.b.(2)(a)).
Parental leave used in more than one increment must be in periods of at least 7 days, for a maximum of 12 increments (see MAX_INCREMENTS).
Parental leave used in more than one increment must be in periods of at least 7 days for a maximum of 12 increments.

Multiple qualifying events occurring within the same 72-hour period are treated as a single event for the allocation of parental leave. Events not within the same 72-hour period are treated as separate events (para 8.b.(2)).
